title: AI Backend Engineer
company_focus: Automotive manufacturer with internal cyber red team
description:
We are an internal Cyber Red Team pioneering the use of
Agentic AI to simulate real-world adversaries. This AI-
driven role will heavily be dependent on automating real-
world attack workflows using Python and LLMs. We are looking
for a strong Python engineer who is curious about security
and wants to work on automating offensive techniques using
AI. Cyber Security background is NOT mandatory. You will
work directly with experienced Red Team professionals to
convert real attack logic into scalable, intelligent
systems.


Responsibilities

RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Build Python-based automation around offensive security
  tools
- Integrate LLM APIs (OpenAI/Anthropic) into real workflows
- Design multi-step workflows (input → tool → AI → decision →
  next step)
- Parse and enforce structured outputs (JSON) from AI models
- Maintain context/state across tasks and executions
- Develop internal APIs and wrappers for tools and services
- Continuously improve reliability (error handling, retries,
  edge cases)
- Collaborate with security experts to translate attack
  techniques into automation

MUST HAVE SKILLS:

- Strong Python experience (3–5 years)
  - Experience in writing robust, production-grade Python. You
    must be deeply comfortable with Object-Oriented
    Programming, decorators, context managers, multi
    threading and async logic.
  - Write custom Python scripts and API wrappers (such as Model
    Context Protocol servers)
  - OOP, clean code, modular design
  - Comfortable debugging real-world systems
- Experience with Docker (containerization)
- API & Backend Fundamentals
  - REST APIs (build and consume)
  - JSON handling, request/response lifecycle
- Hands-on experience with LLM APIs
  - Gemini/OpenAI
  - Able to send prompts, parse structured responses
  - Exposure to LangChain / LangGraph / agent frameworks
- Familiarity with deploying code in GCP (Compute Engine or
  Cloud Run)

Qualifications

ANY

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building AI-powered attack automation
systems, integrating LLM APIs into workflows, designing
multi-step agent-like workflows (input → tool → AI →
decision → next step), and working with LangChain/LangGraph
frameworks. The engineer is directly implementing AI agents
that execute adversarial simulations, not supporting
infrastructure for others.
